title: Convert filter cutoff to normalized frequency
description: Express a cutoff in hertz as a fraction of the sample rate so filter design tools receive the correct normalized value.
body: Use when feeding a cutoff into a design routine that expects normalized frequency. Do not pass a raw hertz value where a fraction of Nyquist is expected, since that mis-specifies the filter entirely. Actions: divide the cutoff by the Nyquist frequency, verify the result lies between zero and one, and compare the resulting response against the intended corner. Validation signals include the simulated minus-three-decibel point matching the requested cutoff and a normalized value within the valid range. Risk boundary: an out-of-range normalized cutoff silently clips or wraps, so deny any value outside zero to one and recompute the scaling.
